phrasing: What makes the wind blow?
explanation: The sun heats some parts of the ground more than others. The warm air over those parts rises up toward the sky. Cooler air near the ground rushes in to take its place. This rushing air is really the wind that we feel. So wind is just air moving from cool spots to warm ones.

phrasing: What makes icebergs stay on the surface of the water?
explanation: A giant block of ice in the sea is called an iceberg. When water freezes, it spreads out and fills a bit more space. This leaves the ice a bit less heavy than the sea water. Because it weighs less, the ice floats up near the top. Most of the ice still hides below the water line.

concept: Why do we feel warm in the sun?
phrasing: How do we get warmth from the sun?
explanation: The sun sends out rays of light that spread in all ways. These rays travel a long way down to the ground. When the rays land on your skin, they turn into heat. That heat soaks into your skin and warms you up. This is the reason the warm sun makes you feel toasty.

phrasing: What makes the weather change?
explanation: The sun warms some parts of the earth more than others. This makes warm air and cool air move all around. The moving air carries clouds, rain, and wind from place to place. As fresh air arrives, the weather begins to change. So changing air is the reason the weather keeps changing.

phrasing: How can fish see when they're in the water?
explanation: Fish have eyes that are made to work under water. Light from above sinks down and spreads through the water. The light bounces off things and goes into their eyes. Their eyes gather the light and send a picture to the brain. This is how a fish can see well while it swims.

phrasing: Can you explain what photosynthesis means?
explanation: Green plants can make their own food using the sunlight. Their leaves soak up the light and mix it with air and water. This mixing makes a sweet food that feeds the growing plant. The plant uses that food to build roots, stems, and leaves. So sunlight is what lets a green plant feed itself.

phrasing: How come we have tears when we're sad?
explanation: Tears come from tiny glands tucked just above your eyes. Big feelings send word to those glands to make more tears. The tears well up and spill over, running down your face. Crying can rinse your eyes and help ease those big feelings. So tears come out when big feelings build up in you.

phrasing: What makes ice slippery?
explanation: A thin layer of water always rests on top of the ice. Your shoe slides on this wet layer with almost no grip. There is little for the bottom of your shoe to hold. So your feet slide away and you lose your footing. That wet layer on top is what makes the ice so slick.

phrasing: What can you find inside an egg?
explanation: Inside an egg you find a yellow part and a clear part. The yellow center, called the yolk, feeds a growing baby bird. The clear jelly around it works to cushion the young bird. A hard shell wraps the whole egg to keep it safe. Together these parts help a young bird grow inside.

concept: How do bubbles form in boiling water?
phrasing: Can you tell me how bubbles start in hot water?
explanation: The water at the bottom of the pot gets very hot. The hot water turns into a gas that we call steam. This gas joins up into little bubbles near the bottom. The light bubbles rise fast through the water to the top. There they pop, and that is the bubbling you see.

concept: Why do stars appear in different patterns?
phrasing: Can you tell me why we see stars in various formations?
explanation: Stars sit in many spots spread far away in space. From the earth they look like tiny bright dots at night. Our eyes join those scattered dots into shapes and lines. Long ago people saw shapes in them and gave them names. So the patterns come from how our eyes link the dots.

phrasing: What is a food chain?
explanation: A food chain shows how food is passed along in nature. Plants make their own food from the bright light of the sun. Small animals come along and munch on those green plants. Big animals then eat the smaller ones for a meal. So food and its power move up along the chain.

phrasing: What causes sunburn in people?
explanation: The sun gives off strong rays that our eyes cannot see. Too much of this ray can burn the skin on your body. Your skin turns red and feels hot, sore, and tender. That red, sore skin is really what we call a sunburn. So we wear hats and shade to guard against strong sun.

concept: How does a seed know when to grow?
phrasing: What tells a seed to start growing?
explanation: A seed rests in the ground and waits for the right time. It feels the warmth around it and drinks up some water. Warmth and water are the signals that spring has come. These signs wake the seed and tell it to start growing. Then a tiny root and shoot push out of the seed.

phrasing: What makes lightning appear during a storm?
explanation: Inside a storm cloud, bits of ice bump and rub together. This rubbing builds up a charge, a lot like static. The charge grows stronger until it jumps out as a spark. That giant spark leaps across the sky as bright lightning. So lightning is a giant spark born high inside the clouds.

concept: What do insects do in the winter?
phrasing: Can you tell me what insects do when it's winter?
explanation: Winter grows far too cold for most bugs to fly around. Many of them hide under loose bark or in the ground. Their little bodies slow right down so they can rest. They stay tucked away through the cold, dark winter days. When warm days come back, the resting bugs wake up again.

phrasing: How does a car move?
explanation: A car burns fuel inside a part that we call the motor. The burning fuel makes a push that turns other parts. Those parts spin a rod that turns the wheels around. The turning wheels grip the road and roll the car ahead. So burning fuel is really what makes the whole car move.

phrasing: Can you explain how electricity moves?
explanation: Power moves as tiny bits that flow inside metal wires. Each little bit gives the next one a small push. This push runs down the wire and moves very fast. In this way the power flows all the way to your lamp. So a moving line of tiny bits carries the power along.

phrasing: Can you tell me how a volcano erupts?
explanation: Deep below the ground, hot melted rock builds up over time. This melted rock is light, so it slowly pushes up. It pushes on the top until the mountain bursts open. The hot melted rock then spills down the sides as lava. So an eruption is hot rock breaking out from below the ground.

concept: How do squirrels find food in the winter?
phrasing: How come squirrels know where to look for food in winter?
explanation: During the fall, squirrels bury nuts all around the ground. They bury the nuts in many small spots to hide them. Their keen sense of smell helps them find the buried nuts. They dig the hidden nuts back up to eat during winter. So a keen nose helps them find the food they hid away.

concept: What are vibrations and how do they make sound?
phrasing: What are vibrations and how do they make sound?
explanation: A quick shake back and forth is what we call a vibration. When a thing shakes, it keeps pushing on the air near it. That pushing travels out through the air as tiny waves. Your ears catch these waves and turn them into sound. So each sound is born when a thing shakes to and fro.

phrasing: Can you explain how we detect different smells?
explanation: Things around you give off tiny bits into the air. You breathe those floating bits high up into your nose. Special spots inside your nose catch and read the bits. They send a quick word to your brain about the smell. Your brain then tells you just what you are smelling.

concept: How do seeds spread out in nature?
phrasing: Why do seeds move away from the plant they come from?
explanation: A plant makes seeds that must find new ground to grow. Some seeds catch the wind and drift far away. Others cling to the fur of animals or get eaten. These rides carry the seeds far from the parent plant. Spreading out gives each seed more room to grow well.

concept: Why do some animals have stripes?
phrasing: What's the reason behind animal stripes?
explanation: Stripes can help a creature hide in the tall grass. The lines blend in and make it harder to be seen. This helps it hide from the things that want to hunt it. Some stripes also mix up a hunter that gives chase. So stripes are a smart trick to help keep them safe.

phrasing: How would you describe pressure?
explanation: A push on something is what we mean by pressure. The harder you press, the more pressure you make. Air and water push on things all the time. The deeper you go, the harder the water pushes on you. So pressure is really just how hard a thing gets pushed.

concept: What is the purpose of soil?
phrasing: What is the purpose of soil?
explanation: Soil is the loose brown dirt that covers the ground. It holds the water and food that plants need to live. Roots grip the soil to hold a plant up straight. Many small bugs and worms make their home in it. So soil feeds the plants and gives them a place to grow.

concept: Why do we have to sleep?
phrasing: What is the reason we need sleep?
explanation: Your body works hard all through the long busy day. Sleep gives your body a chance to rest and mend itself. While you sleep, your brain sorts out all that you learned. In the morning you wake up fresh and full of go. So sleep is the way your body and mind get set again.

phrasing: Can you explain what creates sound?
explanation: Sound is made of tiny waves that move through the air. When a thing shakes, it pushes on the air all around. That push moves out as waves going every which way. The waves travel through the air until they reach you. When those waves reach your ears, you hear it as sound.

concept: Why do we have to drink water?
phrasing: What makes water important for us?
explanation: Your body holds a lot of water deep down inside. You lose a little each time you sweat or cry. Drinking a glass puts that lost water back in you. Your body needs the water to do all of its work. Without enough water, you start to feel weak and tired.
